notes: Catalog for reusable vanilla ES6 mini-app Lego blocks. AIWizard should compose from these modules before generating custom logic. Prefer local bundling into published mini-app ZIPs; CDN loading is only a development option. Schema 1.2.0 adds composition addon recipes (isAddon: true) that layer on top of a base recipe — see recipe.with-leaderboard. Marker shape extended with optional addons: [recipeID, ...]; the gate enforces requiredComponents subset for each addon.

categories.description: Loop, timing, state, motion, and collision primitives shared by many mini-app genres.
Composable gameplay actors such as ships, paddles, cards, boards, tile grids, and player avatars.
Reusable overlays, HUD widgets, and control surfaces that can be re-skinned per swatch.
Swatches, audio, textures, and other shared presentation assets.

components.name: GameManager
components.summary: requestAnimationFrame baseplate with delta time, pause/resume, resize support, events, collision callbacks, and entity lifecycle management.

components.name: Physics2D
components.summary: Simple motion integrator plus AABB and circle collision helpers for lightweight 2D arcade and platformer logic.

components.name: TurnBasedManager
components.summary: Turn, phase, round, history, and rule dispatcher baseplate for board, card, word, and puzzle games.

components.name: Entity
components.summary: Lightweight gameplay base class with motion fields, world wrapping helpers, AABB/circle hit shapes, and reusable lifecycle hooks.

contracts.constructor: new Entity({ x, y, vx, vy, width, height, radius, wrap })

components.name: SpaceShip
components.summary: Asteroids-style player ship with thrust, rotation, drag, wrapping, and projectile spawning.

components.name: Asteroid
components.summary: Jagged drifting asteroid body with spin, size levels, and split helpers for Asteroids-like games.

components.name: Projectile
components.summary: Short-lived arcade projectile with velocity, optional wrapping, and compact vector rendering.

contracts.constructor: new Projectile({ x, y, angle, speed, ttl, wrap })

components.name: Paddle
components.summary: Pong-style paddle with keyboard/AI movement helpers and simple canvas rendering.

components.name: Ball
components.summary: Pong/breakout ball with reset, speed clamp, bounce helpers, and vector rendering.

components.name: GridBoard
components.summary: Reusable tile grid with row/col addressing, neighbor lookup, swapping, world conversion, and optional wrapping.

components.name: Board8x8
components.summary: Chess/checkers board built on GridBoard with algebraic coordinates, square coloring, and piece iteration.

components.name: Card
components.summary: Standard playing card value object with id, color, blackjack value, and readable string output.

components.name: Deck
components.summary: Standard 52-card deck with shuffle, draw, discard, reset, and blackjack hand value helper.

components.name: BlackjackRules
components.summary: Blackjack hand evaluation, dealer hit policy, blackjack/bust checks, and payout settlement.

components.name: PokerHandEvaluator
components.summary: Five-to-seven-card poker hand evaluator with ranking and tie-breakers.

components.name: PlatformerPlayer
components.summary: Platformer avatar with acceleration, gravity, coyote time, jump buffering, facing direction, and grounded state.

components.name: PathFollower
components.summary: Waypoint-following entity for tower defense creeps, Frogger traffic, patrols, and scripted enemies.

components.name: GridMover
components.summary: Grid-snapped movement helper for maze runners, Frogger players, traffic, and tile-based enemies.

components.name: AIPathfinder
components.summary: Breadth-first grid pathfinder for PacMan ghosts, tower-defense path checks, and maze enemies.

components.name: Match3Logic
components.summary: Match-three resolver with horizontal/vertical run detection, clearing, column collapse, refill, and cascades.

components.name: SwipeTileGrid
components.summary: Swipe-merge tile grid for 2048 / Threes / Doodle Merge clones. NxN numeric tile grid with classic 2048 swipe-merge mechanics: each tile merges at most once per swipe, two-of-a-kind become their sum. Includes spawnRandomTile (90% 2 / 10% 4 by default), canMove / isGameOver, isWin (configurable winValue, default 2048), and a static directionFromSwipe helper for pointer-event delta detection.

helpers: static directionFromSwipe(startX, startY, endX, endY, threshold) -> 'left'|'right'|'up'|'down'|null
contracts.state: score: number; bestTile: number; moveCount: number; hasReachedWinValue: bool

components.name: MemoryMatchBoard
components.summary: Memory match (concentration) board. NxM grid (must have even cell count) with face-down/face-up/matched card states. flipCard(r,c) returns {state: 'first'|'match'|'mismatch'|'ignored', card, matchedPair?, mismatchPair?}. Caller delays ~800-1200ms after a mismatch then invokes resolveMismatch() to flip both cards back. Includes static buildShuffledDeck(symbols, totalCells) + Fisher-Yates shuffle helpers. MEMORY_DEFAULT_SYMBOLS exports 36 emoji for default decks.

contracts.state: moves: number; misses: number; matchedCount: number; faceUpCells: [{row,col}]

components.name: SudokuBoard
components.summary: Sudoku 9×9 board with row/column/3×3-subgrid validation. Cells carry {value, locked, notes}. setCell ignores locked clues. validateCell returns conflict-set; isComplete = filled+no-conflicts. Includes static SudokuBoard.generatePuzzle(difficulty) that builds a fresh randomized puzzle (easy=40 clues / medium=32 / hard=26 / expert=22). solve() does backtracking. Pencil-mark notes (Set<1-9>) per cell for the candidate-display UI feature.

components.name: HangmanGame
components.summary: Hangman state machine (letter-by-letter word guessing classic). Tracks guessed letters, wrong count, and win/loss state. guessLetter(L) returns {kind:'correct'|'incorrect'|'repeat'|'ignored', positions?, wrongCount, state, masked}. maskedWord() reveals guessed letters as letters and unguessed as '_'. remainingLetters() returns A-Z minus tried letters for keyboard enable/disable. HANGMAN_DEFAULT_WORDS exports 100 common 4-8 letter words.

helpers: guessLetter(L) -> {kind, letter, positions?, wrongCount, state, masked}
contracts.state: target: string; guessed: Set<letter>; wrongCount: number; state: 'idle'|'playing'|'won'|'lost'; maxWrong: number (default 6)

components.name: TetrisBoard
components.summary: Standard 10×20 Tetris game board with the seven canonical tetrominoes (I/O/T/L/J/S/Z), 7-bag random piece distribution, simple wall-kick rotation, soft/hard drop, and standard line-clear scoring (100/300/500/800 × level). Includes ghostPosition() for the landing-preview UI feature, gravityMs() for level-scaled fall speed, and TetrisBoard.COLORS palette for the 7 piece colors.

contracts.state: score: number; level: number; lines: number; active: {kind,rot,row,col}|null; nextKind: string; gameOver: bool

components.name: SolitaireKlondike
components.summary: Klondike Solitaire game state machine. Standard 7-tableau / 4-foundation / stock+waste / 3-card-flip layout. canStackTableau (alternating colors descending) + canStackFoundation (same-suit ascending) helpers. moveFromWaste/Tableau/Foundation API; autoFinishToFoundations() helper for endgame; undo() with 30-snapshot history. Builds + shuffles a standard 52-card deck. Card type: {suit:'H'|'D'|'C'|'S', rank:1-13, faceUp}. CARD_SYMBOLS exports the Unicode glyphs (♥♦♣♠) + colors.

contracts.state: tableau: [[card]] x7; stock: [card]; waste: [card]; foundations: {H,D,C,S: [card]}; moves: number; score: number; history: [snapshot]

components.name: TileSwapper
components.summary: Selection and adjacent-swap controller for match-three and tile puzzle boards.

components.name: RevealLogic
components.summary: Minesweeper-style mine seeding, adjacency scoring, flag toggling, and flood reveal.

components.name: LetterGrid
components.summary: Word-game grid with row word helpers and Wordle-style correct/present/absent scoring.

components.name: WordDictionary
components.summary: Lightweight dictionary helper for word-trace games. Wraps a word list in a Set for O(1) has() + a prefix Set for trie-prefix pruning during real-time tracing. findAllOnGrid(grid) DFS-enumerates every dictionary word reachable on a WordTraceGrid for hint/score-target generation.

contracts.exported-data: COMMON_WORDS_SAMPLE: ~1000 short English words (3-7 letters) for tests + small mini-apps. Production should bundle 2-10k word list.

components.name: WordTraceGrid
components.summary: Adjacent-letter trace grid for Squaredle / Bookworm / Letter-Blast / WordHunt clones. 4×4 (or NxN) grid; user drags through adjacent cells (8-direction default) to trace a word; commitWord(dictionary) validates against dictionary + min length + already-found tracking.

components.name: MastermindLogic
components.summary: Mastermind secret-code generation and exact/color-only feedback scoring.

components.name: WaveManager
components.summary: Wave spawner for tower defense, enemy attacks, traffic bursts, and level pacing.

components.name: Turret
components.summary: Target-seeking turret with range, cooldown, aim angle, and projectile-factory hook.

components.name: TurretVariants
components.summary: Four turret subclasses with distinct stats: BasicTurret (50g, fast, low damage), CannonTurret (120g, slow, splash damage), FrostTurret (90g, slows enemies), BeamTurret (180g, continuous beam, long range). Each renders with a unique canvas-path vector sprite from VectorSprites. Drop-in replacement for the base Turret when you want meaningful build choice.

factory: new BasicTurret({ x, y, faction, projectileFactory })
new CannonTurret({ x, y, splashRadius?, projectileFactory })
new FrostTurret({ x, y, slowFactor?, slowDuration?, projectileFactory })

components.name: CameraFollow
components.summary: World-camera helper for ship-centered Asteroids variants, platformers, and large maps.

components.name: CheckersRules
components.summary: Checkers diagonal movement, captures, kinging, and move application helpers.

components.name: ChessRules
components.summary: Chess setup, piece movement, capture rules, check filtering, checkmate, and stalemate helpers for 8x8 board games.

contracts.scope: Handles normal piece movement and pawn promotion; castling and en passant can be layered in assembly code.

components.name: TicTacToeRules
components.summary: 3x3 tic-tac-toe rules + inline depth-9 minimax AI. Board is a 9-element array; cells are 'x', 'o', or null. TicTacToeAI.pickMove(board, color) returns the perfect-play move index. Use this instead of MinimaxAI for tic-tac-toe — depth-9 inline is tractable and self-contained.

contracts.scope: Self-contained 3x3 game logic. AI difficulty is 'perfect' (depth-9 minimax) or 'easy' (random legal). For larger boards or harder games, use entities.minimax-ai with a custom evalFn instead.

components.name: MinimaxAI
components.summary: Generic alpha-beta (negamax) search for any 2-player turn-based board game whose rules expose legalMoves + applyMove. Snapshots from-square/to-square/capture before each apply so search-and-undo doesn't clone the board. Default depth 2.

contracts.evalFn: function(board, color) -> number; positive when `color` is winning

components.name: SimpleOpponent
components.summary: Random-legal-move opponent. Use as a fallback when a smarter AI isn't available — satisfies the 'every 2-player game must have an opponent' rule (universal.json u-010).

components.name: ChessAI
components.summary: MinimaxAI wrapper with chess-specific evaluator (material values + central control + king safety). Default depth 2 keeps moves under ~1s.

components.name: CheckersAI
components.summary: MinimaxAI wrapper for checkers — material + king bonus + advancement evaluator. Default depth 3.

components.name: PongAI
components.summary: Beatable AI paddle for Pong-style games. Tracks ball Y with reaction delay (default 110ms) + per-decision error (default 12% of paddle height). Without delay+error a tracking paddle is unbeatable.

contracts.factory: new PongAI({ paddle, ball, bounds?, delay?, error?, deadZone? })

components.name: CloudOpponent
components.summary: Drop-in async opponent that calls window.mChatAI.cloud.llm() for move selection. Same pickMove(board, color) API as ChessAI/CheckersAI/SimpleOpponent — async-only. Falls back to a passed-in local AI on any failure (cloud absent, parse error, timeout, token budget). Pairs with ui/CloudAIToggle for the 'Strong' difficulty tier per wisdom rule bg-007.

contracts.factory: new CloudOpponent({ rules, fallback, promptBuilder, parseMove, timeoutMs?, onError?, onFallback? })

components.name: SpriteRig
components.summary: Multi-part composite sprite framework with attack-animation helpers. A rig is a tree of named parts (head/torso/arm-left/arm-right/...), each with its own draw(ctx) and animatable transform. Includes stock animations: extendArm, flinchHead, whiteFlash, popText, punchCombo. Mandated by ag-016 (multi-part anatomy) + ag-017 (visible attack animations) for Boxing, Joust, fighting games, etc.

contracts.factory: new SpriteRig({ parts: [{ name, draw, anchor?, zOrder? }] })
runtime: draw(ctx, x, y, { scale?, flipX?, alpha? })

components.name: MiniHeader
components.summary: Compact app header (title + optional subtitle) sized to ~50-60px tall. The single source of truth for mini-app titles. Replaces oversized .hero-panel patterns; never write a raw <h1> for a user-visible title — mount MiniHeader instead.

contracts.css: Self-injects #mchatai-mini-header-styles on first instance; safe to instantiate multiple times.

constraints.userVisibleStrings: Never include internal architecture jargon ('Lego', 'web-component', 'mini-app', 'assembled from blocks') in title/subtitle. Use the actual brand or game name only.

components.name: RestartOverlay
components.summary: Centered modal overlay for game-over / play-again. Mobile-first (button ≥ 60×200px), keyboard-bindable (Enter/Space), self-injecting CSS, mounts against any host. Mandatory for every game with a terminal phase per wisdom rule u-029. Marker check `requiresRestartButton: true` enforces presence.

components.name: Leaderboard
components.summary: Global leaderboard wiring — submitFinal(score, metadata), renderRankCard(host, result), renderTopList(host, count). All methods no-op safe when window.mChatAI.leaderboard bridge is absent (offline / signed-out). Mandatory wiring for board games (bg-006) and arcade games (ag-010) with a final score.

static: Leaderboard.submitFinal(score, metadata) -> Promise<{ rank, percentile, played } | null>
Leaderboard.renderRankCard(host, result, options?) -> DOM node | null
Leaderboard.renderTopList(host, count, options?) -> Promise<DOM node | null>
submitAndShowRank(host, score, metadata?, options?) -> Promise<DOM node | null>

components.name: GameOverWithRank
components.summary: Composed game-over panel that wraps Leaderboard.submitFinal + renderRankCard + renderTopList. When the player isn't signed in (submitFinal returns null), renders a 'Sign in at mchatai.com to post your score' CTA instead of a rank card; the top-list still renders for everyone (public read). Restart button included when onRestart is provided. Pairs with ui.leaderboard via recipe.with-leaderboard addon.

static: GameOverWithRank.show({ host, score, metadata?, title?, subtitle?, topCount?, onRestart?, restartLabel?, signIn? }) -> Promise<HTMLElement | null>

components.name: CloudAIToggle
components.summary: Three-button difficulty selector (Easy / Medium / ⚡ Strong). The Strong tier is auto-disabled when window.mChatAI.cloud is unavailable. Pairs with entities/CloudOpponent. Wisdom rule bg-007.

contracts.factory: new CloudAIToggle({ target, defaultLevel?, tokenCost?, tokenCostUnit?, onChange })

components.name: ScoreBoard
components.summary: DOM HUD component for current score and best score, with mChatAI bridge persistence and localStorage fallback.

contracts.dom: Creates a root element and optional mount target for CSS-driven theming.

components.name: SvgShapes
components.summary: Reusable SVG shape renderer for distinct per-token silhouettes (lozenge, hexagon, star, chiclet, circle, diamond, heart, triangle). Drop-in for match-3, mahjong, color-puzzle, sudoku-with-icons, any tile game where N colors share the board. Each color gets a unique shape so the board reads as variety, not a color grid (wisdom rule vq-shape-per-color).

contracts.render: SvgShapes.render({shape, fill, shine?, ariaLabel?, extraClass?}) -> svg HTML string
contracts.renderToken: SvgShapes.renderToken(token) -> svg HTML string for {shape, fill, shine, name}
contracts.defaultTokens: SvgShapes.defaultTokens() -> default 6-color palette tuned for match-3

components.name: HintPulse
components.summary: Idle-aware hint glow controller for tile puzzles. After idleMs of inactivity, pulses a found valid move (caller supplies findHint). Cancel on next tap; expose now() for an explicit Hint button. Wisdom rule game-idle-hint-pulse.

contracts.constructor: new HintPulse({boardEl, findHint, idleMs?, holdMs?, selectorFor?, hintClass?})
contracts.schedule: hints.schedule() — start idle countdown after each successful move
contracts.now: hints.now(holdMs?) — explicit hint button trigger; auto-fades after holdMs
contracts.dispose: hints.dispose() — tear down on game-over / unmount
contracts.css: Call HintPulse.injectCss() once on init for the default .hint pulse keyframes; or override hintClass to bring your own.

components.name: PopBurst
components.summary: Radial light flash for tile-destroyed / matched / cleared feedback. Drop-in for match-3, mahjong, jewel-puzzle, line-clear games. Self-contained CSS via PopBurst.injectCss(); spawn(parentEl) creates and auto-removes the overlay.

contracts.spawn: PopBurst.spawn(parentEl, { ttlMs?, className? }) — returns the overlay node (already attached, auto-removed)
contracts.css: Default .pop-fx class with .pop-fx::before radial gradient; @keyframes popBurst (.5s scale 0.4 → 1.8 + opacity 0)

components.name: SpawnDrop
components.summary: Entry & exit micro-animations for tiles in any grid game. Two CSS classes (.spawn for drop-in, .popping for destroy) with one canonical overshoot curve so every tile game looks juicy without thinking. Auto-removal via SpawnDrop.pop() returns a Promise.

contracts.pop: await SpawnDrop.pop(tileEl) — add .popping, remove from DOM, resolves when done
contracts.css: @keyframes spawnPop (cubic-bezier overshoot) and tilePop (scale + rotate + drop-shadow brightness flash)

components.name: ScoreRise
components.summary: Floating '+50' / 'COMBO ×3' popup that rises and fades over a tile or container. Universal positive feedback for any score event in any genre: tile games, breakout, shoot-em-ups, idle clickers, quiz games. Container needs position: relative or absolute.

contracts.show: ScoreRise.show(parentEl, text, { color?, fontSize?, ttlMs? }) — attaches and auto-removes after ~950ms

components.name: BoardShake
components.summary: Universal 'no-go / wrong answer / illegal move' shake feedback. Works on any container: match-3 board (invalid swap), password input (wrong code), chess square (illegal move), quiz button (wrong answer). Two intensities; auto-cleans the class so subsequent calls retrigger.

components.name: VirtualJoystick
components.summary: Pointer-event virtual joystick overlay for mobile/touch arcade controls.

components.name: PianoKeyboard
components.summary: DOM piano keyboard that emits note/frequency events and can drive AudioManager.

components.name: KeyboardInput
components.summary: Small keyboard binding helper for word games, grid movement, and arcade controls.

components.name: Swatches
components.summary: Design tokens (color + radius + spacing + typography + fonts) and CSS variable helper for re-skinning gameplay HUDs, layout, and canvases without changing logic. Apply once on init; consume tokens via var(--mchat-*) in CSS or this.swatch.tokens.* in canvas paint.

components.name: AudioManager
components.summary: Tiny WebAudio SFX helper for beeps, noise bursts, and named sound effects without external assets.

components.name: SpriteRenderer
components.summary: Pixel-grid sprite renderer for Canvas2D. Each sprite is a 2D array of palette indices (0 = transparent). Supports composite layered sprites, frame-based animation, scale/rotation/flip/anchor. Use this instead of plain colored rectangles for ANY game entity (player, enemy, item, projectile) — see wisdom rule vq-001.

helpers: drawSprite(ctx, grid, palette, { x, y, scale, anchor, rotation, flipX, flipY, alpha })
new Sprite({ grid, palette, scale, animation })

components.name: VectorSprites
components.summary: High-fidelity canvas-path sprite renderers for hero entities — knight, dragon, treasure, key, gate. Use these instead of pixel grids when entities are large enough that detail matters (16x16 pixel grids can't render a recognizable dragon). Each function accepts (ctx, x, y, { scale, flipX, alpha, palette }).

helpers: drawDragon(ctx, x, y, { scale, flipX, palette })
drawGate(ctx, x, y, { scale, unlocked, palette })

components.name: SpritePresets
components.summary: Bundled pixel-art sprites for common entities — knight, dragon, treasure, key, gate, plumber, coin (animated), pipe, brick, goomba, and 4 turret variants. Each comes with a default palette that's overridable. Use these instead of inventing rectangles or simple shapes — wisdom rule vq-001 + vq-002 (3+ visually distinct entities) is satisfied automatically.

compositionRecipes.name: Open-World Asteroids Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Asteroids expanded into a camera-following open-world space shooter
assemblyNotes: Use CameraFollow and world coordinates instead of wraparound screen coordinates.
Use Turret for moon/base installations and Projectile for both player and enemy fire.
Keep Asteroid splitting from the normal Asteroids recipe, but spawn/despawn objects around the camera viewport.

compositionRecipes.name: Asteroids / Top-Down Arcade Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Asteroids-style vector arcade shooter
assemblyNotes: Write glue code around the shipped engine and entity modules instead of rewriting movement, wrapping, projectile, and scoring logic from scratch.
For open-world Asteroids variants, keep SpaceShip and Projectile but disable world wrapping and add camera/world coordinates in the assembly layer.

compositionRecipes.name: Defender Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Defender-style horizontal scrolling rescue shooter
assemblyNotes: Build a recognizable Defender clone: horizontal wrap or long-scrolling world, ship always free-flying, enemy abductors, rescue targets/humans, radar/minimap, lives, score, and escalating waves.

Use GameManager, Physics2D, Projectile, CameraFollow, ScoreBoard, Swatches, and AudioManager as the baseplate. Only generate the game-specific enemy wave, radar, terrain, and rescue glue until dedicated Defender blocks are promoted.
Instantiate GameManager with an options object like new GameManager({ canvas, width, height, onUpdate, onDraw }); never call new GameManager(ctx).

compositionRecipes.name: Pong Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Pong-style paddle ball arcade game
assemblyNotes: Use Paddle and Ball as fixed rules primitives; only generate scoring rules, input binding, and theme.
Use GameManager callbacks (`onUpdate`, `onDraw`) and `game.start()`; there is no GameManager.update() API.
Use PongAI to drive the CPU paddle. Without delay+error parameters a perfect ball-tracker is unbeatable; defaults (110ms / 12%) make it fun.

compositionRecipes.name: Frogger Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Frogger-style road and river crossing arcade game
assemblyNotes: Use GridBoard for lanes, GridMover for the player, and PathFollower for cars/logs; generate only lane data and collision rules.

compositionRecipes.name: Pac-Man Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Pac-Man-style maze chase arcade game
assemblyNotes: Use GridBoard for maze walls and pellets, GridMover for player/ghost movement, and AIPathfinder for ghost chase/scatter behavior.
Instantiate documented component constructors directly; there is no framework-level initialize() API.

compositionRecipes.name: Side-Scrolling Platformer Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Mario-style side-scrolling plumber platformer or Q*bert-style pyramid hopper
assemblyNotes: Build a recognizable side-scrolling platformer clone: player, platforms, gaps, coins, hazards, lives, score, camera follow, keyboard/touch controls, and restart.

Render the player with PLUMBER (or KNIGHT for adventure-flavored), coins with COIN_FRAMES animation, pipes with PIPE, bricks with BRICK, enemies with GOOMBA — all from resources/SpritePresets.js. Plain colored rectangles for player/enemies are forbidden (vq-001).

For Pyramid Hopper variants, use a hex-staircase background pattern + KNIGHT player + GOOMBA enemies. The platformer recipe is the right home for this — there is no separate `recipe.pyramid-hopper` (yet).
Use PlatformerPlayer for movement/jumping and CameraFollow for side scrolling; do not omit the mchatai-web-components-used marker.

compositionRecipes.name: Tower Defense Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Desktop Tower Defense / Bloons-style path defense game
assemblyNotes: Build a recognizable tower defense clone: visible path, build pads, turrets, enemy waves, projectiles, lives, gold, score, and Start Wave control.

Use TurretVariants — BasicTurret / CannonTurret / FrostTurret / BeamTurret — to give the player real build choices. A single turret type is boring; the four variants have distinct stats (cost, range, cooldown, damage, splash, slow) so players plan placement.
Render each turret with its bundled sprite (TURRET_BASIC, TURRET_CANNON, TURRET_FROST, TURRET_BEAM from SpritePresets). Do NOT render turrets as plain circles — wisdom rule vq-001 forbids it.
Add a small turret-shop UI with one button per variant showing cost; selecting a variant before clicking a build pad determines what gets placed.
Instantiate documented component constructors directly; there is no framework-level initialize() API.

compositionRecipes.name: Wordle Clone / Word Quest
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Wordle-style five-letter word guessing game with six guesses and colored feedback
assemblyNotes: Build Word Quest as a Wordle clone, not a generic word builder: one hidden five-letter answer, six rows of five cells, Enter/Delete/New Word controls, green/yellow/gray feedback, and colored QWERTY keyboard keys.
Use LetterGrid for guesses and feedback; generate dictionary/level content separately from grid scoring.

Adopt effects/ Legos for visual feedback: BoardShake.shake(row) on invalid guesses, ScoreRise.show(row, '+points STREAK') on correct. Each effect Lego exports an injectCss() helper — call once on init so @keyframes are present before the first event. A wordle without invalid-shake or score-rise reads as broken even when logic is correct.

compositionRecipes.name: Squaredle / Bookworm Letter-Trace
assemblyNotes: Use WordTraceGrid for the 4×4 (or NxN) board. Listen to pointerdown/pointermove/pointerup with `passive: false` and call grid.startTrace / extendTrace / commitWord. (See mobile-frontend wisdom mob-005.)
Bundle a 2-10k word dictionary inline as a JS array; pass it to WordDictionary. For tests, use the exported COMMON_WORDS_SAMPLE (~1000 words).
Use WordDictionary.findAllOnGrid(grid) at puzzle generation to compute the FULL set of legal words for that letter arrangement. Use the count + total possible score as the level target.
Highlight adjacent cells visually. Use isPrefix(currentWord) during pointermove to color-cue the user when their trace can still complete a valid word.
Mobile-portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001..016). Touch targets ≥ 44×44 logical pixels.
Persist foundWords + best score in localStorage. Show 'X / Y words found' progress. End-of-puzzle bonus screen lists missed words via WordDictionary.findAllOnGrid.

compositionRecipes.name: Mastermind Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Mastermind-style code breaker with color pegs and exact/color-only feedback
assemblyNotes: Use MastermindLogic for code generation and feedback pegs; do not ask the model to reinvent exact/color-only scoring.
Use ScoreBoard.setScore(), ScoreBoard.add(), or ScoreBoard.reset(); there is no ScoreBoard.update() API.
On win OR turns-exhausted, call RestartOverlay.show() with a clear title (e.g. 'Code cracked!' / 'Code locked'). Wire onRestart to newGame().
On win, submit the final score via Leaderboard.submitFinal(score, { result, turns, codeLength }) and renderRankCard against a #rankCard host.

compositionRecipes.name: Minesweeper Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Minesweeper-style grid reveal puzzle
assemblyNotes: Use RevealLogic for mine seeding, adjacency counts, flags, and flood reveal; generate board size/difficulty settings only.
On win OR mine-hit, call RestartOverlay.show() with title/subtitle. On win, submit final score via Leaderboard.submitFinal(score, { result, time_seconds, mines, rows, cols }).

compositionRecipes.name: Match-Three Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Bejeweled / Candy Crush-style swap-and-cascade match-three puzzle
assemblyNotes: Use TileSwapper for selection/swap input and Match3Logic for matches/cascades; generate tile skins and level goals only.
Do not stop at an import shell. A valid match-three app must render a visible tile board, selectable adjacent swaps, match/cascade resolution, score/combo HUD, and restart/shuffle controls.

Adopt the effects/ Legos for visual feedback: SpawnDrop for tile entry/exit, PopBurst on every match, ScoreRise on score events, BoardShake on invalid swaps, HintPulse for idle hints. Each effect exports an injectCss() helper — call once on init so the @keyframes are present. A board with no animations on match/swap/score events should be treated as missing required juice, not 'minimal-by-design'.

compositionRecipes.name: 2048 / Threes Swipe-Merge
assemblyNotes: Use SwipeTileGrid for the 4×4 tile grid (size: 4 default, or pass size: 5/6 for harder variants).

Mobile-first: detect swipe via pointerdown + pointerup, NOT keyboard. Use SwipeTileGrid.directionFromSwipe(startX, startY, endX, endY, 30) to convert deltas to direction. (See mobile-frontend wisdom mob-005.)
Desktop fallback: also bind ArrowUp/ArrowDown/ArrowLeft/ArrowRight to grid.swipe(direction).
After every successful swipe (result.moved == true), call grid.spawnRandomTile() to add a new 2 or 4. Track grid.score for the scoreboard.
Use effects.spawn-drop for new-tile entrance animation and effects.score-rise for merge feedback (per merge points).
Color palette: tiles get distinct colors per power. Use a logarithmic gradient (2 = lightest → 2048+ = boldest accent). Pull from resources.swatches.
End-of-game: when grid.isGameOver(), show overlay with final score + best tile + 'Try Again' button (mac-022 web equivalent — visible-restart).
On grid.isWin() === true (first time the win-value tile appears), show a 'You won! Keep playing?' overlay; let the user continue past 2048.
Persist best score + best tile in localStorage. Show end-of-game vs personal-best comparison ONLY at game-over, not in-play (per high-score-is-end-of-run-chrome wisdom).
Mobile-portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001..016). Touch targets ≥ 44×44 logical pixels.

compositionRecipes.name: Memory Match / Concentration
assemblyNotes: Use MemoryMatchBoard for the card grid. Pick 4×4 (16 cards / 8 pairs) for kids/easy, 4×6 (24 / 12 pairs) for medium, 6×6 (36 / 18 pairs) for hard.

Call MemoryMatchBoard.buildShuffledDeck(symbols, rows * cols) once per new game. MEMORY_DEFAULT_SYMBOLS has 36 emoji; substitute with country flags / animal photos / chess-piece glyphs / etc. for thematic variants.

Touch UX (mob-005): listen to pointerup on each card element, NOT click. Call grid.flipCard(r, c). The 'state' return value tells you what to do: 'first' (just flip); 'match' (apply matched class to both, play match SFX); 'mismatch' (apply face-up briefly, then setTimeout(800ms) → grid.resolveMismatch() → re-render); 'ignored' (no-op).
Animate card flips with CSS transform: rotateY(180deg) and a face-back / face-front structure. Touch targets ≥ 44×44 logical pixels (mob-002).
Score model: track grid.moves (pair-attempts) and grid.misses. Final score = (totalPairs / moves) * 1000 OR a time-based scheme. Show end-of-game stats: pairs found / moves / misses / time elapsed.
End-of-game overlay (mac-022 web equivalent): grid.isComplete() → show 'Try Again' button + 'New Theme' button (lets user pick a different symbol set).
Persist best-moves and best-time in localStorage per board size.
Mobile-portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001..016).

compositionRecipes.name: Sudoku 9×9 Logic Puzzle
assemblyNotes: Use SudokuBoard for the 9×9 grid. Generate puzzles via SudokuBoard.generatePuzzle('easy'|'medium'|'hard'|'expert') — caller doesn't need a hard-coded puzzle list.
Render with 3×3 subgrid borders (heavier 2px lines every 3 rows/cols). Locked clue cells get distinct styling (bolder font + slightly darker bg).

Touch UX (mob-005): pointerup to select a cell; show a 1-9 number pad below the grid (touch targets ≥ 44×44 per mob-002). Caller maps tap of a number → grid.setCell(activeR, activeC, value); validate immediately and highlight conflicts via grid.validateCell.
Pencil-mark mode: a toggle button switches input from 'commit value' to 'toggle note'. Notes display as small 1-9 candidates inside the cell.

Highlight features (huge UX boost): when a cell is selected, soft-highlight (a) same row, (b) same column, (c) same 3×3 subgrid, (d) all cells with the same value. Conflict cells get red highlight via allConflicts().
Win detection: after every setCell, if grid.isComplete() show overlay with elapsed time + difficulty + 'New Puzzle' button (mac-022 web equivalent — visible-restart).
Persist current puzzle in localStorage so refresh doesn't lose progress. Save: JSON.stringify(grid.cells).
Mobile-portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001..016).
Difficulty selector (segmented control or pills): easy/medium/hard/expert. Each picks a different generatePuzzle clue count.

compositionRecipes.name: Hangman Word-Guess
assemblyNotes: Use HangmanGame for state. Pick a fresh word from HANGMAN_DEFAULT_WORDS (or a thematic substitute) on each round. Caller-controlled vocabulary ⇒ easy to swap in animals / movies / foods / countries.

Render 3 surfaces: (1) the gallows + hangman drawing (SVG with paths revealed by wrongCount: head=1, body=2, lArm=3, rArm=4, lLeg=5, rLeg=6); (2) the masked word as widely-spaced underscores/letters; (3) an A-Z on-screen keyboard.
On-screen keyboard touch UX (mob-005): pointerup per key. Disable already-guessed letters (or grey them out + apply correct/incorrect color). Use grid.remainingLetters() to drive enabled state.
Color cues: correct guesses = green letter on the keyboard; incorrect = red. Animate the masked word's letter when it gets revealed.
End-of-round overlay (mac-022 web equivalent): if state==='won' show 'You won!'; if state==='lost' show 'Game over' + the word that was hidden. Both overlays have a 'New Word' button.
Persist streak / longest streak in localStorage. Show streak in the header.
Touch targets ≥ 44×44 logical pixels (mob-002). Mobile-portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001..016).

compositionRecipes.name: Tetris Falling Blocks
keywords: I O T L J S Z
assemblyNotes: Use TetrisBoard for the 10×20 grid (or pass rows: 22 / cols: 10 for the standard 'extra rows above' rendering).
Game loop: setInterval(() => grid.tick(), grid.gravityMs()) — re-call setInterval whenever lines clear (level changes → faster gravity).

Mobile touch input (mob-005): pointerdown to detect tap, pointerup with delta to detect swipe (left/right = move, down = soft drop, up = rotate, swipe-up-fast = hard drop). Bottom on-screen control bar with arrow buttons + rotate as a fallback.
Desktop keyboard: ArrowLeft/Right = move, ArrowDown = softDrop, ArrowUp = rotate, Space = hardDrop, P = pause.
Use TetrisBoard.ghostPosition() to render the 'landing preview' (semi-transparent piece silhouette where the current piece would land). Standard mobile-Tetris UX feature.
Render the 'next' piece in a side-panel using TetrisBoard.TETROMINOES[grid.nextKind].rotations[0].

Scoring is built-in (100/300/500/800 × level). Level rises every 10 lines automatically. Use effects.score-rise for combo / line-clear visual feedback. Use effects.board-shake for tetris (4-line) clears.
End-of-game overlay (mac-022 web equivalent) when grid.gameOver: show final score + lines + level + 'New Game' button.
Persist best score in localStorage. Show in header.
Mobile-portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001..016). Touch targets ≥ 44×44 logical pixels.

compositionRecipes.name: Klondike Solitaire
assemblyNotes: Use SolitaireKlondike for ALL game state and rules. Drag-and-drop UX is the standard solitaire interaction model — listen to pointerdown on a card, pointermove to drag, pointerup to drop on a tableau column or foundation. The state machine handles all rule validation; UI just calls grid.moveFromWaste/Tableau/Foundation and reads the {ok, reason} return.
TAP-TO-AUTO-MOVE for mobile: a single tap on a card should attempt to move it to its foundation if legal (saves the user a long drag). Use canStackFoundation to check.
Render face-down cards as a card-back pattern (gradient or pattern). Face-up cards show rank+suit using CARD_SYMBOLS.
Tableau layout: stack cards with vertical offset (~25-30px on mobile) so multiple cards in a column are visible. Top card is fully exposed.
Foundation cells start empty; show a faint suit-symbol watermark per pile so the player knows which is which.
Stock + waste in the top-right (or top-left for mobile-portrait). Drawing 3 cards: visually fan the top 3 of the waste so the player can see all 3.
Auto-complete button (effects.score-rise feedback): when no face-down cards remain in the tableau, an 'Auto Finish' button calls grid.autoFinishToFoundations() to play out the win.
End-game overlay (mac-022 web equivalent) when grid.isWon(): show 'You Won!' + final score + moves + 'New Game' button.
Persist best score in localStorage. Show in header.
Mobile-portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001..016). Touch targets ≥ 44×44 logical pixels (whole card surface, not just the visible portion).

compositionRecipes.name: Blackjack Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Blackjack / Twenty-One card game
assemblyNotes: Use BlackjackRules for hand totals, dealer policy, blackjack checks, and settlement; generate table flow and UI only.
Use ScoreBoard.setScore(), ScoreBoard.add(), or ScoreBoard.reset(); there is no ScoreBoard.update() API.

compositionRecipes.name: Texas Hold'em Poker Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Texas Hold'em poker game

compositionRecipes.name: Checkers Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Checkers / Draughts board game
assemblyNotes: Use CheckersRules for diagonal movement, captures, and kinging; generate board UI and turn prompts only.
Wire CheckersAI to play one color (default: 'black'). After every human move, schedule the AI's reply via setTimeout (~350ms for thinking-feel). Never let the user play both sides.
Do not output markerless board-game HTML. Render a visible 8x8 board, selectable pieces, legal move highlights, capture feedback, turn HUD, and reset control.

compositionRecipes.name: Chess Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: standard playable Chess board game
assemblyNotes: Use ChessRules for standard setup, legal move generation, check filtering, checkmate, and stalemate.
Wire ChessAI to play one color (default: 'black'). After every human move, schedule the AI's reply via setTimeout (~350ms for thinking-feel). Never let the user play both sides.
ChessAI default depth is 2 (move under ~1s on typical hardware); raise to 3 for stronger play if you can afford up to ~3s thinking time.
Do not invent lifecycle APIs such as initialize(). Render a visible 8x8 board, selectable pieces, legal move highlights, turn/status HUD, and reset control.

compositionRecipes.name: Tic-Tac-Toe vs AI
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Tic-Tac-Toe / Noughts and Crosses with single-player AI
assemblyNotes: Tic-tac-toe is the H3.1 acceptance test for the H2.7 universal lifts. Wire RestartOverlay, Leaderboard, and CloudAIToggle from the start — they're inexpensive on a 3x3 game and prove the contract for harder games.

Use entities.tic-tac-toe-rules for the rules + AI. It exports TicTacToeRules, TicTacToeAI (perfect-play depth-9 minimax inline), and emptyBoard(). Do NOT roll your own rules engine — the catalog component is depth-9-perfect and battle-tested in the example.
Don't use entities.minimax-ai here — TicTacToeAI is already optimal at depth 9 and self-contained. MinimaxAI is for larger search trees (chess, checkers).
After human move: ai.pickMove(board, 'o') → rules.applyMove(board, move, 'o') → rules.winner(board) → if game-over, RestartOverlay.show() + Leaderboard.submitFinal().
Score deltas: +100 for perfect-difficulty win, +50 for easy win, +25 for draw. Loss = 0 (don't submit). Adjust if you want stricter ranking math.
Inline 3x3 board as 9 buttons (CSS grid 3x3). Click handler dispatches to game.handleHumanMove(index). Easier than a generic GridBoard for this size.

compositionRecipes.name: Piano Keyboard Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: playable piano keyboard / synth toy
assemblyNotes: Build a recognizable compact piano clone: visible piano keys, click/tap play, keyboard shortcuts, current note, note trail, and synth feedback.
Use PianoKeyboard for DOM keys and KeyboardInput for hardware shortcuts; do not output an import-only shell.

compositionRecipes.name: NYT Mini Crossword Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: NYT Mini-style compact crossword puzzle with across/down clues
assemblyNotes: Build a recognizable compact crossword clone: small square grid, black cells, numbered starts, Across/Down clue lists, selected cell/word highlighting, keyboard entry, timer, score, and persistent best time.
Until dedicated CrosswordGrid and ClueSet blocks are promoted, keep the crossword puzzle data small and explicit in glue code.
Instantiate documented component constructors directly; there is no framework-level initialize() API.

compositionRecipes.name: Atari Adventure Clone
compositionRecipes.cloneTarget: Atari Adventure-style room maze with keys, dragons, treasures, and locked gates
assemblyNotes: Build a recognizable Atari Adventure clone: room-to-room map, player avatar, keys, locked gates, treasure goal, simple enemies, inventory status, score/time, and reset.

Render the player as KNIGHT, the dragon as DRAGON, the treasure as TREASURE, the key as KEY, the locked gate as GATE — all bundled in resources/SpritePresets.js. Do NOT use plain colored rectangles or triangles for entities (wisdom rule vq-001).

Aim for at least 6-8 distinct rooms, not 3. Each room can mix terrain (forest, cavern, keep, vault, bridge, river) by reskinning the bg color and item layout. The lego-style draw layer makes adding rooms cheap.
Until dedicated room graph and inventory blocks are promoted, model rooms and items as small data tables in glue code.
Instantiate documented component constructors directly; there is no framework-level initialize() API.

compositionRecipes.name: Add Hosted Leaderboard (addon)
assemblyNotes: This is an ADDON recipe. It LAYERS ON TOP of the base game recipe (Snake, Pong, Tetris, etc.) — never replaces it. The marker MUST be `{recipe: <base-recipe-id>, addons: ["recipe.with-leaderboard"], components: [...all base + addon components...]}`.

ON GAME OVER (and ONLY on game-over — see u-018 + score-show-personal-best-on-game-over), call: `await GameOverWithRank.show({ host, score: finalScore, metadata: { level, moves, duration_ms }, onRestart: () => game.restart() });` This single call submits the score, renders the rank card OR a 'Sign in at mchatai.com' CTA (when not signed in), renders the top-10, and mounts a Play Again button.

Do NOT call `Leaderboard.submitFinal` mid-game. Do NOT show rank or top-list while gameplay is active — that violates wisdom rule score-show-personal-best-on-game-over and u-hud-minimalism-meaningful-only.

Do NOT manually fetch /api/leaderboards/* or any HTTP endpoint. ALWAYS go through the Leaderboard / GameOverWithRank components. They handle bridge availability, sign-in state, network errors, and offline fallback transparently.

When `Leaderboard.submitFinal` resolves null (player not signed in OR bridge absent OR offline), the component renders a persuasive 'Sign in at mchatai.com to post your score' CTA. This is BY DESIGN — the leaderboard is the reason to create a free mChatAI account. Do not suppress the CTA or replace it with a generic 'leaderboard unavailable' message.
The board-id is supplied by the host page (window.mChatAI.leaderboard bridge) — the generated game never sets it. v1 → v2 of the same published project share the same board.
Mobile-first: GameOverWithRank.show() is a single panel that fits 360px wide; do not stack multiple custom panels around it.

absorptionProcess: Select a known-good mini-app from catalog/good-games.json or /admin/template-candidates.
Extract reusable mechanics into a focused ES module with a documented constructor and small method surface.
Reassemble the source game as an example using imports from this catalog.
Run module import checks and browser smoke tests.
Mark the component ready in this manifest only after the reassembled example behaves like the source game.

goldenAssemblies.name: Asteroids Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Runnable Asteroids example composed from the shared engine, entity, swatch, and HUD modules.
notes: Use this as the canonical wiring example for how GameManager, SpaceShip, Asteroid, Projectile, ScoreBoard, and Swatches fit together.
Preserve the module boundaries when generating variants. Change rules, UI, theme, or enemy mix before collapsing the code back into one giant game loop.

goldenAssemblies.name: Word Quest Wordle Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Runnable Wordle clone branded as Word Quest, composed from turn manager, letter grid, keyboard input, scoreboard, swatches, and audio modules.
notes: Use this as the canonical wiring example for a Wordle clone: visible 5x6 grid, clickable QWERTY keyboard, Enter/Delete/New Word buttons, dictionary validation, green/yellow/gray feedback, streak, score, and persistent best score.
Do not stop at importing LetterGrid and KeyboardInput. Generated variants must render visible cells and real clickable controls that call game actions.

goldenAssemblies.name: Minesweeper 99 Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Runnable Minesweeper clone composed from turn manager, grid board, reveal logic, scoreboard, swatches, and audio modules.
notes: Use this as the canonical wiring example for a Minesweeper clone: visible 9x9 grid, click reveal, right-click or long-press flagging, timer, score, persistent best score, and new game control.
Generated variants should compose with RevealLogic for mine seeding, adjacency counts, flags, and flood reveal instead of reimplementing those mechanics.

goldenAssemblies.name: Candy Match Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Runnable Candy Match clone composed from turn manager, grid board, tile swapper, match-3 logic, scoreboard, swatches, and audio modules.
notes: Use this as the canonical wiring example for a Candy Match clone: visible 8x8 tile grid, adjacent click/tap swapping, match detection, cascades, combos, moves, score, persistent best score, shuffle, and new-board controls.

Generated variants should compose with TileSwapper.trySwap(), Match3Logic.findMatches()/clearMatches()/collapseColumns(), GridBoard.swap(), TurnBasedManager.record(), and ScoreBoard.add()/reset(). Do not output a shell that only imports these modules.

goldenAssemblies.name: Mastermind Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Runnable Mastermind clone composed from turn manager, grid board, mastermind logic, keyboard input, scoreboard, swatches, and audio modules.
notes: Use this as the canonical wiring example for a Mastermind clone: visible guess history, hidden/revealed code row, clickable color palette, Submit/Delete/New Code controls, exact/color-only feedback pegs, turn tracking, score, and persistent best score.
Generated variants should compose with MastermindLogic.scoreGuess() and ScoreBoard.add()/reset(); do not invent APIs such as scoreboard.update().

goldenAssemblies.name: Pong Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Runnable Pong clone composed from the shared game loop, physics helpers, paddle, ball, scoreboard, swatches, and audio modules.
notes: Use this as the canonical wiring example for a Pong clone: GameManager owns the requestAnimationFrame loop, onUpdate moves paddles/ball, onDraw paints the court, and ScoreBoard.add()/setScore()/reset() updates persistent best score.
Generated variants should compose with Paddle.move(), Ball.reset()/bounceX()/bounceY(), and Physics2D.intersectsAABB(). Do not invent APIs such as gameManager.update().

goldenAssemblies.name: Blackjack Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Runnable Blackjack clone composed from turn manager, deck, card, blackjack rules, scoreboard, swatches, and audio modules.
notes: Use this as the canonical wiring example for a Blackjack clone: visible dealer/player hands, Deal/Hit/Stand/Double controls, hand totals, phase/bet/deck HUD, bankroll score, persistent best bankroll, and dealer settlement.
Generated variants should compose with Deck.draw()/reset(), BlackjackRules.handValue()/shouldDealerHit()/settle(), and ScoreBoard.setScore(). Do not invent APIs such as scoreboard.update().

goldenAssemblies.name: Checkers Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Runnable Checkers clone composed from turn manager, Board8x8, CheckersRules, swatches, and audio modules.
notes: Use this as the canonical wiring example for a Checkers clone: visible 8x8 board, standard opening setup, selected-piece highlight, legal diagonal moves, captures, king promotion, turn tracking, move/capture HUD, and reset control.
Generated variants should compose with CheckersRules.legalMoves(), CheckersRules.applyMove(), Board8x8.isDarkSquare(), and TurnBasedManager.nextTurn(). Do not emit markerless monoliths.

goldenAssemblies.name: Chess Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Runnable Chess clone composed from turn manager, Board8x8, ChessRules, swatches, and audio modules.
notes: Use this as the canonical wiring example for a Chess clone: visible 8x8 board, standard setup, piece selection, legal move highlights, captures, turn tracking, check/checkmate/stalemate status, and reset control.

Generated variants should compose with ChessRules.setupStandardBoard(), ChessRules.legalMoves(), ChessRules.applyMove(), Board8x8.cellToAlgebraic(), and TurnBasedManager.nextTurn(). Do not invent APIs such as initialize().

goldenAssemblies.name: Tic-Tac-Toe Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Runnable Tic-Tac-Toe vs AI clone composed from MiniHeader, RestartOverlay, Leaderboard, CloudAIToggle, TurnBasedManager, TicTacToeRules + TicTacToeAI (perfect-play depth-9 minimax), swatches, and audio modules.
notes: Use this as the canonical wiring for a Tic-Tac-Toe vs AI clone: 3x3 grid as 9 buttons, click handler dispatches game.handleHumanMove(index), perfect-play AI from TicTacToeAI(depth=9 minimax), RestartOverlay on terminal phase, Leaderboard score submit, CloudAIToggle for difficulty.

Generated variants must import { TicTacToeRules, TicTacToeAI, emptyBoard } from './web-components/entities/TicTacToeRules.js'. Do NOT inline a custom rules engine — TicTacToeAI is depth-9 perfect and self-contained.

Wire: human click → rules.applyMove(board, idx, 'x') → rules.winner(board) → if not terminal, ai.pickMove(board, 'o') → rules.applyMove(...) → rules.winner(board) → if terminal, RestartOverlay.show() + Leaderboard.submitFinal().

goldenAssemblies.name: Piano Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Runnable piano keyboard clone composed from piano keyboard, keyboard input, audio manager, and swatches modules.
notes: Use this as the canonical wiring example for a Piano clone: visible piano keys, tap/click controls, A-K keyboard shortcuts, current note HUD, note count, melody trail, play-scale control, and synth feedback.
Generated variants should compose with PianoKeyboard.play(), PianoKeyboard.noteFrequency(), KeyboardInput.bindMany(), and AudioManager.beep(). Do not output a shell that only imports these modules.

goldenAssemblies.name: Pac-Man Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Runnable Pac-Man clone composed from game loop, grid board, grid movers, pathfinder, scoreboard, joystick, swatches, and audio modules.
notes: Use this as the canonical wiring example for a Pac-Man clone: visible maze, walls, pellets, power pellets, ghost chase/scatter, score, lives, persistent best score, keyboard controls, touch joystick, and restart control.
Generated variants should compose with GridBoard, GridMover, AIPathfinder.nextStep(), GameManager callbacks, ScoreBoard.add()/reset(), and VirtualJoystick. Do not invent APIs such as initialize().

goldenAssemblies.name: Tower Defense Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Runnable tower defense clone composed from game loop, grid board, path followers, projectiles, wave manager, turrets, scoreboard, swatches, and audio modules.
notes: Use this as the canonical wiring example for a Tower Defense clone: visible path, build pads, click-to-place turrets, wave spawning, enemy path following, projectile hits, lives, gold, score, persistent best score, and Start Wave control.

Generated variants should compose with GameManager callbacks, WaveManager.update(), PathFollower enemies, Turret projectileFactory, Projectile, Physics2D.intersectsCircle(), and ScoreBoard.add()/reset(). Do not invent APIs such as initialize().

goldenAssemblies.name: Platformer Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Runnable side-scrolling platformer clone composed from game loop, physics helpers, grid board, platformer player, camera follow, scoreboard, joystick, swatches, and audio modules.
notes: Use this as the canonical wiring example for a Platformer clone: visible side-scrolling stage, platforms, gaps, coins, hazards, lives, score, persistent best score, camera follow, keyboard controls, touch joystick, and restart control.

Generated variants should compose with PlatformerPlayer.setInput()/landOn(), CameraFollow.follow()/apply(), Physics2D.intersectsAABB(), ScoreBoard.add()/reset(), and VirtualJoystick. Preserve the recipe marker.

goldenAssemblies.name: Mini Crossword Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Runnable NYT Mini-style crossword clone composed from turn manager, grid board, keyboard input, scoreboard, swatches, and audio modules.
notes: Use this as the canonical wiring example for a Mini Crossword clone: visible 5x5 grid, black cells, clue numbers, Across/Down clue lists, selected cell, keyboard entry, timer, check/reset controls, score, and persistent best score.
Generated variants should compose with GridBoard, KeyboardInput.bindMany(), TurnBasedManager, ScoreBoard.setScore()/reset(), and AudioManager. Do not invent APIs such as initialize().

goldenAssemblies.name: Adventure Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Runnable Atari Adventure clone composed from game loop, grid board, grid mover, pathfinder, scoreboard, swatches, and audio modules.
notes: Use this as the canonical wiring example for an Atari Adventure clone: room-to-room navigation, player square, key pickup, locked gate, dragon hazard, treasure goal, inventory HUD, score, persistent best score, and restart control.

Generated variants should compose with GridBoard, GridMover.snapTo(), AIPathfinder.nextStep(), GameManager callbacks, ScoreBoard.add()/reset(), and AudioManager. Do not invent APIs such as initialize().

goldenAssemblies.name: Defender Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Runnable Defender clone composed from game loop, physics helpers, entity base, projectile, camera follow, scoreboard, swatches, and audio modules.
notes: Use this as the canonical wiring example for a Defender clone: side-scrolling ship, long world, enemy abductors, rescue humans, radar strip, projectiles, waves, lives, score, persistent best score, and restart control.

Generated variants should compose with GameManager({ canvas, width, height, onUpdate, onDraw }), Entity, Projectile, Physics2D.intersectsCircle(), CameraFollow.follow()/apply(), ScoreBoard.add()/reset(), and AudioManager. Do not invent APIs such as new GameManager(ctx).

goldenAssemblies.name: Frogger Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Runnable Frogger clone composed from the shared game loop, grid board, grid mover, path follower, scoreboard, virtual joystick, swatches, and audio modules.
notes: Use this as the canonical wiring example for a Frogger clone: visible road and river lanes, grid-snapped player movement, moving cars, moving logs, homes, lives, score, persistent best score, keyboard controls, touch joystick, and restart control.
Generated variants should use GridBoard for row/column mapping, GridMover for the frog, and PathFollower for cars/logs. Do not output a shell that only imports these modules.
